emergent_themes.description: When extreme lifestyle circumstances (4-5 hour daily commute) create genuine dependency on delivery services as survival mechanism rather than convenience choice
Gap between self-perception as 'money-conscious saver' and actual spending behavior (€140/week on food, 80% on takeaways)
Completely different food behaviors and identities based on location - delivery-dependent in Dublin vs family-oriented cooking at home
Sophisticated optimization across multiple platforms based on restaurant type, promotions, and specific use cases
Subscriptions enabling higher order frequency which justifies continued subscription - self-reinforcing cycle

coding_summary: P006 represents an extreme case of delivery dependency driven by unsustainable commuting lifestyle. Key findings: 1) Orders 5+ times per week in Dublin due to 4-5 hour daily commute and couch-surfing accommodation, 2) Spends €140/week on food (80% takeaways) while claiming to be 'money-conscious', 3) Exhibits sophisticated multi-platform optimization strategy, 4) Shows clear location-based food identity split between Dublin delivery dependency and home family cooking, 5) Demonstrates subscription-enabled ordering cycle, 6) Plans to cook more post-graduation but expects continued grocery delivery use, 7) Has rider experience providing unique dual perspective on platform economics.

coverage_gaps.reason: Participant shows no evidence of stigma around solo ordering - orders alone 5+ times per week without apparent social concern
